input:
"#CONTEXT#
You are a B2B sales strategist writing a GTM intelligence memo for a sales team. You will receive information about:
1. A client company whose product is being sold
2. A lead who previously worked at a company that is a known customer of the client
3. The lead's current company where they now hold a relevant role

The lead's past company is a known customer of the client. The lead held a relevant role there and would have been exposed to or aware of the client's product. They have since moved to a new company in a similar or more senior role."

"#INPUTS#
client_company_name: Nostra
client_company_domain: nostra.ai
client_company_description: Nostra helps the world’s leading ecommerce brands future-proof their digital infrastructure with cutting-edge edge technology. We enable high-growth DTC companies to deploy enterprise-grade infrastructure in minutes, not months. Our edge platform is designed to accelerate site speed, increase conversion rates, and drive more revenue, while protecting your brand from modern threats. With products like Edge Delivery Engine, Edge Protection, Edge ID, and Edge Crawler, we give brands the tools to: • Boost performance by 20–30% • Block ad bot traffic before it hits your site • Extend cookie life for better attribution • Optimize content for LLM indexing and AI-native search Brands like Jolie, Amiri, and Olukai rely on Nostra to deliver faster, safer, smarter ecommerce experiences

lead_full_name: Yoon Chung
lead_linkedin_url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yoonchungdesign
lead_current_job_title: CEO/ Founder
lead_current_company_name: Ona by Yoon Chung
lead_current_company_domain: onawear.com
lead_current_company_description: ONA is a high fashion, high performance brand committed to delivering directional pieces that reflect a modern women's lifestyle. Founded in 2019 by Yoon Chung, the brand occupies a category all on its own, merging couture-inspired design with technical fabrics that are easy to care for and maintain. The collection is available on onawear.com. For more information, please visit us online or on Instagram: @onabyyoon

lead_past_company_name: Forever 21
lead_past_company_domain: forever21.com
lead_past_company_description: Forever 21 continues to be a fashion industry leader with a mission to make the latest trends accessible to all. F21 persists on staying ahead of innovation and providing styles and fit that our customers love. While reinventing for now and beyond, we’re seeking passionate and creative new family members to join us on this adventure! F21 continues to operate E-commerce and 450 locations across the U.S. along with maintaining a strong brand DNA with consumers and within the fashion industry. 

In February 2020, Forever 21 came under new ownership made up of Simon Property Group, Brookfield Property Partners, and Authentic Brands Group. Under this new ownership F21 has the financial strength and long term view that will prepare us for the future, while continuing to set the same high standards as it always has. Join our team and learn the inner sanctum of the global fashion industry, while we build the new Forever 21! 

Forever 21 is operated by SPARC Group LLC.
 
About SPARC Group LLC
SPARC Group LLC offers operational and strategic expertise including retail and e-commerce management, sourcing, design and technology for leading global brands. SPARC Group is a joint venture between Simon Property Group, a preeminent retail real estate company and Authentic Brands Group, a global leader in brand development. In addition to Forever 21, SPARC Group brands include Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Nautica, Eddie Bauer, and Lucky Brand.
 
SPARC Group LLC is an equal opportunity employer, and is committed to maintaining a workplace free from prohibited employment conduct, including discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, domestic partner status, civil partnership, status as a covered veteran, status in the Uniformed Services of the United States, citizenship and any other characteristic protected by law.
lead_past_company_job_title: Divisional Merchandise Manager - Accessories"

"#OBJECTIVE#
Generate a 5-section GTM strategy memo that helps a sales rep understand why this lead is a high-value target right now and exactly how to approach them.

#RESEARCH DIRECTIVES#
Before writing the memo, research the following:

1. **Current company business context** — What is the current company's scale, market position, ecommerce footprint, and growth trajectory? What operational or technical challenges would a company like this face? What about their stage creates urgency for the client's product?

2. **Competitive landscape** — Who are the current company's direct competitors? What market pressure do they face? How does site performance, conversion optimization, or digital infrastructure factor into their competitive position?

3. **Lead's role and authority** — Based on their title and the company context, what does this person own? What decisions do they influence or control? Why are they the right person to talk to about the client's product?

4. **The alumni signal** — The lead previously held a relevant role at a company that uses the client's product. What would they have seen or experienced there? How does that experience translate to their current situation?

5. **Complexity delta** — How do the challenges at the current company compare to the past company? Are they dealing with more brands, more SKUs, more markets, more traffic, more complexity? Frame the current situation relative to what they've already navigated.

#OUTPUT FORMAT#
Write a 5-section memo in sharp, conversational prose. No bullet points. No headers beyond the section names. Each section should be a tight paragraph.

**OVERVIEW**
2-3 sentences. Who is this person, what is their current role, and what is their connection to the client's customer base. State the alumni signal clearly.

**MARKET POSITION**
One paragraph. The current company's business context — scale, growth stage, competitive pressure, and why their digital infrastructure matters right now. What is at stake for them?

**RELEVANCE & READINESS**
One paragraph. Why this specific person. What did they see or own at their past company? What do they own now? Why would they recognize the value of the client's product immediately?

**RECOMMENDED APPROACH**
One paragraph. The specific angle for outreach. Include a sample message or talk track that references the alumni connection and speaks to the current company's situation. Make it concrete, not generic.

**TAKEAWAY**
One sentence. The verdict — why this lead matters and the urgency level.

#STYLE GUIDANCE#
- Be direct and specific. No filler, no hedge words.
- Do not fabricate metrics, quotes, or claims. Only infer plausibly from the inputs and your research.
- Do not claim the lead directly used or implemented the client's product unless you have evidence. Frame it as exposure and awareness based on their role.
- The memo should read like it was written by someone who understands the market, not by an AI summarizing inputs.
- Total length: 300-400 words.

Output Schema

  ""overview"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""2-3 sentences. Who is this person, their current role, and their alumni connection to the client's customer base.""
  },
  ""market_position"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on the current company's business context, competitive pressure, and why digital infrastructure matters now.""
  },
  ""relevance_and_readiness"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on why this specific person — what they saw at the past company, what they own now, why they'd recognize value immediately.""
  },
  ""recommended_approach"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph with the specific outreach angle and sample messaging that references the alumni connection.""
  },
  ""takeaway"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One sentence verdict on why this lead matters and urgency level.""
  }
}"

overview.current_role: Founder/CEO at ONA by Yoon Chung
overview.alumni_signal: Yoon Chung is the Founder/CEO of DTC brand ONA and was previously a Divisional Merchandise Manager at Forever 21. This creates a strong alumni signal, as Forever 21 is a publicly cited customer of Nostra, with a detailed success story highlighting significant improvements in site speed and crawl efficiency. Yoon's senior role at Forever 21 implies she would have had awareness of major site performance initiatives.

market_position.company_context: ONA by Yoon Chung is a Los Angeles-based, founder-led DTC womenswear brand established in 2019. Operating as a lean, early-stage company with 2-10 employees, it positions itself in the 'high fashion, high performance' niche. The business model is focused on direct ecommerce and brand building, indicating a sharp focus on maximizing online growth and profitability.
market_position.ecommerce_footprint: The company's primary sales channels are its own website, onawear.com, and its Instagram profile, which has a modest but engaged following of approximately 7,800. Technographic analysis confirms the use of a modern DTC stack, including payment solutions and Google Analytics, which underscores its reliance on a functional and performing ecommerce platform to drive all of its business.
market_position.competitive_pressure: ONA operates in the highly saturated 'technical-fashion' market, competing with digitally native brands like ADAY and established players such as Alo, Varley, and Tory Sport. In this segment, a premium digital experience is table stakes. Competitors invest heavily in site speed and user experience, meaning ONA faces constant pressure to maintain a fast, responsive, and high-converting website to capture and retain customers.
market_position.infrastructure_urgency: For a boutique DTC brand like ONA that relies on paid social and organic discovery, digital infrastructure is not a luxury but a critical growth lever. Every basis point in conversion rate, every millisecond of page load time, and every dollar of ad spend directly impacts the bottom line. Optimizing Core Web Vitals, ensuring clean traffic for accurate attribution, and improving organic discoverability are urgent priorities to achieve profitable growth and compete effectively.

relevance_and_readiness.past_role_experience: As Divisional Merchandise Manager for Accessories at Forever 21, Yoon Chung operated within a massive ecommerce and retail environment. She would have had a commercial perspective on how site performance, traffic quality, and conversion rates directly impacted revenue at a very large scale, managing a key category for a global fashion giant.
relevance_and_readiness.awareness_of_problem: In her senior merchandising role at Forever 21, she would have been exposed to the constant cross-functional challenges of managing a high-traffic ecommerce site, including the negative business impact of slow page loads, traffic spikes during promotions, and the financial drain of bot traffic on analytics and ad spend.
relevance_and_readiness.awareness_of_solution_impact: Given Nostra's public success story with Forever 21—delivering a 59% acceleration in page load times and a 166% increase in crawl speed—Yoon would have been aware of the significant positive outcomes that enterprise-grade performance solutions can deliver. She has seen firsthand how technical improvements translate into commercial success.
relevance_and_readiness.current_role_authority: As the Founder and CEO of ONA, Yoon is the ultimate decision-maker. Her interview statement that she personally handles everything from design to 'maintaining the website' confirms she has direct control over the digital experience and the authority to approve technology purchases. She is not just a budget holder; she is the hands-on owner of the problem Nostra solves.

recommended_approach.outreach_angle: The core strategy is to leverage her past exposure to Nostra's impact at Forever 21 and reframe it for her current reality as a founder. The message should acknowledge the difference in scale but emphasize that the underlying principles of site speed, conversion, and traffic quality are even more critical for a growing DTC brand where every click and customer counts.
recommended_approach.sample_message_hook: "Hi Yoon, I saw you're building ONA after your time at Forever 21. While you were there, our team at Nostra was working with F21's digital team to significantly boost site speed and crawl efficiency. I imagine at ONA, where every conversion matters, site performance is an even more critical lever for growth."
recommended_approach.value_proposition: Connect Nostra's value directly to the challenges of a founder-led DTC brand. Emphasize how our Edge Delivery Engine can immediately lift conversion rates on your paid and social traffic, how Edge Protection can stop ad bots from draining your marketing budget, and how our crawler optimization can improve your organic discovery—all critical for profitably scaling ONA.
recommended_approach.call_to_action: Propose a brief, 15-minute call to share how we apply the same enterprise-grade principles that helped Forever 21 to high-growth DTC brands like yours. The goal is to offer a founder-friendly path to infrastructure that drives revenue from day one.

takeaway.justification: This is a rare opportunity to engage a founder with direct purchasing power who has prior awareness of our solution's impact at an enterprise scale and now runs a growing DTC brand facing the exact challenges our product is built to solve.

citations.title: How Forever 21 Improved Website Performance & Traffic with Nostra AI
excerpts: Section Title: Forever 21 Accelerates Website Performance and Boosts Organic Traffic with Nostra AI

Forever 21 faced site speed challenges that impacted user experience and conversion rates, such as high bounce rates from slow page loads, reduced user engagement, and difficulties handling traffic surges during peak times. These speed issues also created SEO challenges, as search engines rank slower sites lower and have trouble crawling and indexing them efficiently. Core Web Vitals like Largest Contentful Paint (LCP), First Input Delay (FID), and [Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)](https://www.nostra.ai/blogs-collection/cumulative-layout-shift-cls-how-to-measure-optimize-cls) are negatively affected by poor site performance, particularly on mobile devices.

Forever 21 relies heavily on its online presence to reach its target audience. As digital channels become increasingly important, the performance and speed of Forever 21's website and mobile app are critical for ensuring a seamless customer experience. Partnering with Nostra, a company specializing in web performance optimization, significantly enhanced Forever 21's digital platforms.
The impact of Nostra AI's solutions on Forever 21's website speed and efficiency was remarkable. The slide deck presentation highlights the following key results:

59%
Reduction in Time to First Byte (TTFB)
59% acceleration in page load
166% increase in crawl speed
roduction) **going greener:** This indicates that Forever 21's website is meeting Google's standards for user experience, which is a crucial factor in search engine rankings.

excerpts: With enterprise customer momentum including fashion retailer Forever 21, Nostra AI’s Edge Delivery Engine™ showcases immediate impact on core website metrics

The Nostra AI Edge Delivery Engine™ speeds up websites by examining personalized content such as shopping carts and determining what content can be cached to decrease load times. The engine, paired with Nostra AI’s Crawler Optimization that improves website experiences for bots, provides a powerful suite of tools that drive near-instant results for enterprise retailers like Forever 21 and some of the fastest-growing e-commerce brands like Jones Road, Jolie, BRUNT Workwear, Glamnetic, Cozy Earth and Nood.

“When it comes to e-commerce, it’s no secret that website speed, especially on mobile devices, has a direct impact on conversion rates and revenue,” said Nostra AI CEO and Founder Arthur Root. “Our proprietary Edge Delivery Engine™ makes websites substantially faster, instantly — customers are seeing north of a 10% lift in revenue by simply decreasing page load time.”

Additional excerpts detailing specific performance improvements (reductions in Time to First Byte, faster page loads, improved crawl speed) reinforce the tangible impact Nostra has had on Forever 21’s digital infrastructure, which the lead could recognize given her experience managing e-commerce and performance initiatives at Forever 21. Other excerpts recountForever 21’s online presence and business momentum with Nostra, supporting the idea that the alumni signal is strong and relevant to a current leadership context at a fashion-forward e-commerce brand. While some items focus on general Forever 21 marketing or broader company narratives, they still corroborate that Forever 21 was a customer and that Nostra’s platform contributed meaningful speed and SEO improvements, which would be salient to someone evaluating infrastructure investments at a new company with similar scale and traffic.
